Project Notes — Acme Widget Toolkit

These notes capture some implementation details discussed during the Acme
Widget Toolkit (AWT) design review on April 1.

The reactive Reactor uses topic-based subscriptions. Subscribers receive the
most recent value on attach. Backpressure is intentionally not handled at the
Reactor layer — consumers must throttle if needed.

Themes are pure data — a theme is just a dict of token => value pairs. We
considered making them classes but rejected that to keep theming serializable.

Widget instances are cheap; do not pool them. Profiling on the Acme reference
device showed allocation cost was negligible compared to layout cost.
